current_wind.description: Light breeze from the Northeast, with gusts up to 11.2 mph.

wind_info.description: Light breeze from the left at 11 o'clock.

bh.shot_strategy: With a light 11 o'clock wind from your left, throw a controlled hyzer with a stable fairway driver to keep the disc's flight plate from being exposed, ensuring it stays on line towards the basket and avoids the OB to the right.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled RHFH with a stable fairway driver or approach disc will effectively counter the left-to-right push from the 11 o'clock wind, allowing for accurate placement towards the basket while staying clear of the OB to the right.

bh.shot_strategy: The 11 o'clock wind from your left can complement your natural fade, making a straight shot or slight hyzer with a neutral-to-understable driver a viable option to reach the basket, keeping it away from the OB to the right.

fh.shot_strategy: For control, a LHFH with a stable approach disc can be used to guide the disc accurately towards the basket, mitigating the effect of the 11 o'clock wind and avoiding the OB to the right.

wind_info.description: Light to moderate headwind from 12 o'clock.

bh.shot_strategy: Facing a 12 o'clock headwind at 7.7 mph, use an overstable driver or fairway driver for a straight flight and to prevent the disc from turning over, ensuring it lands safely before the OB to the north.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled RHFH with an overstable disc will be effective against the headwind, allowing you to maintain accuracy and keep the disc from getting pushed too far north and out of bounds.

bh.shot_strategy: With a 12 o'clock headwind, an overstable disc thrown with a hyzer will fight the wind and fade reliably, helping you manage the distance to the basket and stay within bounds to the north.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH with an overstable disc can also be used effectively against the headwind, providing a predictable flight path that accounts for the wind's push and avoids the OB to the north.

wind_info.description: Light breeze from the left at 1 o'clock.

bh.disc_recommendation: Neutral to Slightly Overstable Fairway Driver
bh.shot_strategy: With a light 1 o'clock headwind, a neutral to slightly overstable fairway driver thrown with a clean hyzer will help achieve the desired flight to the basket, minimizing any unwanted turn.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with a neutral disc can be used to combat the light headwind, allowing for a controlled flex shot or a straight flight towards the basket.

bh.shot_strategy: A straight shot with a neutral disc will be effective against the light 1 o'clock wind, allowing for a predictable flight to the basket.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH with a neutral disc can also be used to navigate the light headwind, ensuring a controlled approach to the basket.

wind_info.description: Light breeze from the left at 1 o'clock.

bh.shot_strategy: Facing a light 1 o'clock headwind on this short hole, a neutral fairway driver or stable mid-range with a hyzer release will ensure a straight flight to the basket, resisting any turn from the wind.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled RHFH with a neutral disc can be used against the light 1 o'clock headwind, providing a straight flight to the pin.

bh.shot_strategy: A straight throw with a neutral mid-range or fairway driver will work well against the light 1 o'clock headwind, allowing for an accurate approach to the basket.

fh.shot_strategy: Use a neutral disc with a LHFH to control the flight against the light 1 o'clock headwind, aiming for a straight approach to the basket.

wind_info.description: Crosswind from the right at 8 o'clock.

bh.shot_strategy: With an 8 o'clock crosswind from your right, a hyzer flip with a neutral or understable driver will allow the disc to fight the wind and land softly on the island, avoiding the penalty stroke.

fh.shot_strategy: A controlled RHFH with a stable disc can be used to cut across the fairway and land on the island, but be mindful of the wind pushing right.

bh.shot_strategy: The 8 o'clock wind from your right will push your natural fade even further right, making this a difficult shot; opt for a LHFH with an overstable disc to fight the wind and land on the island.

fh.shot_strategy: Throw a LHFH with an overstable disc to counter the strong push from the 8 o'clock wind, ensuring the disc lands safely on the island and avoids the one-stroke penalty.
